policy_principles: Policy definition is centralized in this registry; enforcement can happen at edge, proxy, API, mesh, and data layers.
mTLS or service identity answers who may call; rate, concurrency, cache, and budget policies answer how much work the system may accept.
Public anonymous routes are never confidential surfaces; they may expose only minimized derived data and must not expose raw, private, business-verified, contact, or bulk-export data.
Browse/read paths may use derived serving indexes and short cache TTLs; authoritative write, permission, and payment decisions must not rely on stale cache.
Public map routes must not expose bbox or bounds request shapes.
